code.text: MRI Brain Without And With Contrast

conclusion:
1. Numerous foci of predominantly cortical restricted diffusion within the cerebral hemispheres bilaterally, largest involving the right parietal, posterior temporal, and occipital lobe with additional foci in the bilateral frontal, parietal, and occipital lobes. There may be tiny foci within the left superior cerebellum as well. There may also be a focus of restricted diffusion at the left basal ganglia/deep white matter.
2. This exam is largely nondiagnostic for metastases given significant motion artifact on postcontrast sequences. No abnormal intracranial mass effect detected.
3. Numerous punctate foci of parenchymal susceptibility, a few of which are new from prior. These are suggestive of microhemorrhages, nonspecific but history of anticoagulation and thrombocytopenia is noted. It would be difficult to exclude hemorrhagic micrometastases given limitations of postcontrast sequences.

Given nondiagnostic postcontrast sequences, repeat contrast MRI recommended to assess for metastases in the future, when clinically indicated/feasible.
